reasoned_about_structure.evidence: Treated lr and bs as separable: held bs=128 fixed and swept lr exps 5-11. One isolated bs=2048 probe at exp 12 was never followed up; no reasoning about joint lr-bs interaction.

formed_tested_hypotheses.evidence: Did form a local hypothesis ('higher lr lowers loss at bs=128') and tested it monotonically, but the hypothesis space was confined to a single bs slice.

exploration_quality.evidence: Froze batch size at 128 and swept lr. Never probed the high-lr + large-bs region; landed in the confident-wrong minimum at lr~1.38e-3.

bifurcation_point: Deciding after exp 4-5 to fix bs=128 and only sweep lr, rather than varying both axes.
bifurcation_reasoning: By exp 2-3 the agent had seen bs=256,512,128,64 give differing losses (evidence bs matters) yet chose to freeze bs and sweep lr alone. With the information in hand it should have probed bs jointly; a reasoning error, not an information gap.
justification: A textbook freeze-one-axis failure: a clean but one-dimensional sweep stopping at the dead-end minimum (regret 0.0163). The pivotal decision to fix bs=128 was avoidable given the variation already observed.
